Blog

Why we rewrote our parser

Last quarter we shipped a complete rewrite of our config parser. This post explains why and what we learned.

The original problem

Our v1 parser was built when the codebase was 12 KLOC. It made three assumptions that didn't survive growth:

  • Configs are always under 1 KB.
  • Schema is fully known at parse time.
  • Errors can be best-effort recovered.

By v3 of the product, none of these were true. We kept patching around the parser instead of through it.

The new design

The rewrite is a streaming tokenizer + walker, modeled on Go's encoding/json internals.

Take what works. json handles malformed input gracefully without panicking, and that was exactly what we needed.

Numbers

Throughput improved 8×. Memory dropped 60%. Lines of code went up 15% but the new ones are testable.

Comments? Open an issue.
